evals.prompt: Prowlarr stopped working after last night's maintenance. Diagnose the cause and tell me what happened, but do not change or restart anything.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads the routine operations reference, checks local repo status and relevant intent, uses drift/status/maintenance logs/container state and Prowlarr logs to establish the cause, and reports evidence without deploying, restarting, or editing anything.

evals.prompt: Add Uptime Kuma to my home server. Keep it Tailscale-only, persist its data, include important state in backups, deploy it, and verify it works.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks the whole working tree, chooses an intentional lifecycle boundary, uses a pinned tag@digest image, binds the UI to the Tailscale IP, stores data outside the rsync-owned tree, adds the bind to host/data-layout.tsv with deliberate ownership and backup policy, and updates state/service documentation. It validates through SOPS and scripts/check.sh, reviews for secrets, commits and pushes the clean artifact before deployment, uses a full deploy because data-layout is a host change, verifies the endpoint and scripts/verify.sh plus drift, and avoids unrelated ingress changes.

evals.prompt: Retire Beszel from the server, but preserve all of its data in case I change my mind.
evals.expected_output: The agent inventories Beszel's homepage, ingress, health, credential, backup, documentation, and data dependencies; brings the compose project down while its deployed definition still exists and without --volumes; removes stale repo references; preserves bind data and named volumes; and keeps the retired state under an explicit backup=yes data-layout row or a validated backed-up export. It runs checks, commits and pushes the clean artifact before deploying affected shared or host scope, and verifies no orphaned route, container, or drift remains.
